atomicity.message: Staged payload is exactly one docs-only dogfooding findings-log update adding A23 and C19-C21 plus backlog and next-ID maintenance.

conditions.message: Docs-only staged payload is whitespace-clean, hash-bound, and paste-safety checked.
evidence: go run ./cmd/burpvalve hash --staged --json matched sha256:bdde462c226769f002eaf42a2818a2ea630a2c38bde8d9f80946ac03fdf67e23 and included only docs/dogfooding-findings-2026-07.md.
git diff --cached --check exited 0.
Staged-text rg scan found the intended new headings and next-ID line; no new bare arrow/redirection findings appeared, and the only $status match is pre-existing B7 text.

conditions.message: The docs-only update appends the requested findings and backlog rows directly in the existing findings log structure without adding a parallel log or unrelated abstraction.
evidence: git diff --cached --name-status showed exactly one staged file: docs/dogfooding-findings-2026-07.md.
Diff inspection found A23, C19, C20, C21, D-backlog rows, and Log discipline next IDs; no code, config, workflow, or tracker state is staged.

Clean temp repo from HEAD plus only git diff --cached --binary reproduced staged hash sha256:bdde462c226769f002eaf42a2818a2ea630a2c38bde8d9f80946ac03fdf67e23 and manifest hash sha256:00e2a9854cb8dcbdde3e942841f405f13b196fd8b3ebe884380dd9f8c0fc8df5; git diff --cached --check passed.

conditions.message: The new findings record limitations and process risks as candidates rather than claiming fixes.
evidence: A23 states ci --commit does not yet provide universal historical parity.
C20 records the closed-before-commit violation instead of hiding it.
C19/C21 are process-fix/tooling-candidate entries, not implemented behavior claims.

evidence: git diff --cached --name-status shows only M docs/dogfooding-findings-2026-07.md.
go run ./cmd/burpvalve hash --staged --json confirms that single included path.

conditions.message: The new findings record source, observation, why it matters, and candidate status.

conditions.message: The staged payload stays within the requested dogfooding findings log scope and does not widen into implementation, tracker, or workflow edits.
evidence: git diff --cached --stat reported only docs/dogfooding-findings-2026-07.md with 97 insertions and 1 deletion; .beads/issues.jsonl is dirty but unstaged and outside this payload.

Diff inspection confirmed A23 covers ci --commit historical multi-cluster audit gap, C19 covers .beads reservation duration, C20 covers closed-before-commit violation, C21 covers unstaged slice durability, and D-backlog/next-ID updates reference those same items.

Clean rg scan found the intended headings and next: A24; it also surfaced only pre-existing $status and harmless inline <sha>/<bead> placeholders, with no new bare arrow chains or shell-redirection examples in the added text.

evidence: C21 discusses stash/worktree slice loss as a finding and suggests durability practices, but does not add executable destructive operations.
git diff --cached --name-status confirms only the findings doc is staged.

The payload does not modify permissions, hooks, workflows, configs, installer behavior, network behavior, or authentication boundaries.
